title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. revolutum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. revolutum, also known as the curly bracken fern or revolute bracken, is a subspecies of the Pteridium aquilinum plant, a type of fern native to various parts of the world. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Pteridium aquilinum subsp. revolutum are attributed to its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as an infusion, or made into a tea, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
